creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Meta will record employees’ keystrokes and use it to train its AI models
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: Meta says that it has a new internal tool that is converting mouse movements and button clicks into data that can train its AI models.
contentSnippet: Meta says that it has a new internal tool that is converting mouse movements and button clicks into data that can train its AI models.

creator: Lucas Ropek
title: (No AI) Unauthorized group has gained access to Anthropic’s exclusive cyber tool Mythos, report claims
dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
content: Anthropic told TechCrunch it is investigating the claims, but maintains that there is no evidence that its systems have been impacted.
contentSnippet: Anthropic told TechCrunch it is investigating the claims, but maintains that there is no evidence that its systems have been impacted.

title: (No AI) SpaceX cuts a deal to maybe buy Cursor for $60 billion
author: Richard Lawler
content:
![Photo of rocket launch with SpaceX logo.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/05/STKB355_SPACEX_C.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

With an [IPO looming for Elon Musk's SpaceX / xAI / X combo platter of companies](https://www.theverge.com/science/915244/spacex-ipo-trillion-dollar-commercial-iss-nasa-launch), SpaceX has announced an odd arrangement to either acquire the automated programming platform Cursor for $60 billion or pay a fee of $10 billion. Buying this startup that's focused on AI coding could help xAI's tools compete with market leader Anthropic, as well as the other competitors. A report by _[The Information](https://www.theinformation.com/articles/google-creates-strike-team-improve-coding-models)_ this week said Sergey Brin has directed Google's "strike team" to [help its agentic AI tools catch up](https://www.theverge.com/tech/914996/sergey-brin-said-google-needs-to-catch-up-to-anthropic-on-ai-coding-agents), while Sam Altman reportedly declared [a "code red"](https://www.theverge.com/news/836212/openai-code-red-chatgpt) at OpenAI last year before shutting down Sora to focus on the ChatGPT superapp and its own [Codex](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/913034/openai-codex-updates-use-macos) too …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/science/916427/spacex-cursor-potential-deal-acquisition)
contentSnippet:
With an IPO looming for Elon Musk's SpaceX / xAI / X combo platter of companies, SpaceX has announced an odd arrangement to either acquire the automated programming platform Cursor for $60 billion or pay a fee of $10 billion. Buying this startup that's focused on AI coding could help xAI's tools compete with market leader Anthropic, as well as the other competitors. A report by The Information this week said Sergey Brin has directed Google's "strike team" to help its agentic AI tools catch up, while Sam Altman reportedly declared a "code red" at OpenAI last year before shutting down Sora to focus on the ChatGPT superapp and its own Codex too …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: With an IPO looming for Elon Musk's SpaceX / xAI / X combo platter of companies, SpaceX has announced an odd arrangement to either acquire the automated programming platform Cursor for $60 billion or pay a fee of $10 billion. Buying this startup that's focused on AI coding could help xAI's tools compete with market […]

title: (No AI) AI backlash is coming for elections
author: Lauren Feiner
content:
![Graphic photo illustration of a voting sign that reads “Vote here”.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25334821/STK466_ELECTION_2024_CVirginia_C.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Ask Americans how they feel about AI and [most say](https://www.ipsos.com/en-us/what-americans-agree-about-ai) they have concerns. Communities have mounted resistance to data center projects, stalling them across the US. On social media, anger at AI companies and executives is unrestrained - sometimes to the point of condoning [violence](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/911778/ai-violence-sam-altman-home).

But look at the issues that most campaigns are focused on, and AI is far less prevalent, experts say.

[More than 60 percent](https://www.ipsos.com/en-us/what-americans-agree-about-ai) of both Republicans and Democrats polled by Ipsos earlier this year agree that the government should regulate AI for economic stability and public safety, and that the technology's development should slow down. Still, "when you just ask folks, 'w …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/policy/916210/ai-midterm-elections-data-centers-jobs)
contentSnippet:
Ask Americans how they feel about AI and most say they have concerns. Communities have mounted resistance to data center projects, stalling them across the US. On social media, anger at AI companies and executives is unrestrained - sometimes to the point of condoning violence.
But look at the issues that most campaigns are focused on, and AI is far less prevalent, experts say.
More than 60 percent of both Republicans and Democrats polled by Ipsos earlier this year agree that the government should regulate AI for economic stability and public safety, and that the technology's development should slow down. Still, "when you just ask folks, 'w …
Read the full story at The Verge.

On social media, anger at AI companies and executives is unrestrained - sometimes to the point of condoning violence. But look at the issues that most campaigns are focused […]

title: (No AI) Scaling Codex to enterprises worldwide
content: OpenAI launches Codex Labs, partners with with Accenture, PwC, Infosys, and others to help enterprises deploy and scale Codex across the software development lifecycle, and hits 4M Codex WAU.
contentSnippet: OpenAI launches Codex Labs, partners with with Accenture, PwC, Infosys, and others to help enterprises deploy and scale Codex across the software development lifecycle, and hits 4M Codex WAU.

title: (No AI) OpenAI helps Hyatt advance AI among colleagues
content: Hyatt deploys ChatGPT Enterprise across its global workforce, using GPT-5.4 and Codex to improve productivity, operations, and guest experiences.
contentSnippet: Hyatt deploys ChatGPT Enterprise across its global workforce, using GPT-5.4 and Codex to improve productivity, operations, and guest experiences.

title: (No AI) Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
content: Our newest audio model introduces granular audio tags that give you precise control to direct AI speech for expressive audio generation.
contentSnippet: Our newest audio model introduces granular audio tags that give you precise control to direct AI speech for expressive audio generation.

title: (No AI) Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
content: Gemini Robotics ER 1.6: Enhancing spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding for autonomous robotics.
contentSnippet: Gemini Robotics ER 1.6: Enhancing spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding for autonomous robotics.

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) OpenAI Beefs Up ChatGPT’s Image Generation Model
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: The ChatGPT Images 2.0 model is here. Our testing shows it’s better at creating more detailed images and rendering text, but it still struggles with languages other than English.
contentSnippet: The ChatGPT Images 2.0 model is here. Our testing shows it’s better at creating more detailed images and rendering text, but it still struggles with languages other than English.

creator: Lily Hay Newman
title: (No AI) Mozilla Used Anthropic’s Mythos to Find and Fix 271 Bugs in Firefox
dc:creator: Lily Hay Newman
content: The Firefox team doesn’t think emerging AI capabilities will upend cybersecurity long term, but they warn that software developers are likely in for a rocky transition.
contentSnippet: The Firefox team doesn’t think emerging AI capabilities will upend cybersecurity long term, but they warn that software developers are likely in for a rocky transition.
